message:
feat(frame): add loc/iloc boolean mask, slice, and row accessors for Series and DataFrame; add groupby as_index=false support

Series — new public methods:
  - loc_bool(&[bool]): label-based boolean mask selection matching
    pandas series.loc[bool_array]. Validates mask length matches series
    length, returns rows where mask is true, preserving index labels.
  - iloc_bool(&[bool]): position-based boolean mask selection matching
    pandas series.iloc[bool_array]. Delegates to loc_bool since for
    Series the two are semantically identical (single axis).
  - loc_slice(Option<&IndexLabel>, Option<&IndexLabel>): label-based
    inclusive-endpoint slice matching pandas series.loc[start:stop].
    Uses position() for start and rposition() for stop to handle
    duplicate labels correctly (first start, last stop). Returns empty
    series when start is after stop.
  - iloc_slice(Option<i64>, Option<i64>): position-based exclusive-end
    slice matching pandas series.iloc[start:stop]. Supports negative
    positions resolved from the end (Python semantics). Returns empty
    series for out-of-range positions.

DataFrame — new public methods:
  - loc_bool(&[bool]): boolean mask row selection matching
    df.loc[bool_array]. Validates mask length, collects positions
    where mask is true, delegates to take_rows_by_positions.
  - iloc_bool(&[bool]): position-based boolean mask row selection,
    delegates to loc_bool (mask selects rows by position either way).
  - loc_slice(Option<&IndexLabel>, Option<&IndexLabel>): label-based
    inclusive-endpoint row slice matching df.loc[start:stop]. For empty
    results, constructs a zero-row DataFrame preserving column schema
    and dtype information.
  - iloc_slice(Option<i64>, Option<i64>): position-based exclusive-end
    row slice matching df.iloc[start:stop]. Handles negative positions,
    preserves column schema on empty results.
  - loc_row(&IndexLabel): return a single row as a Series by label,
    matching df.loc[label] for scalar labels. The Series index is the
    DataFrame column names, and values are the row data. Mixed dtypes
    (e.g., Int64 + Utf8) fall back to Utf8 representation matching
    pandas object dtype behavior.
  - iloc_row(i64): return a single row as a Series by position,
    matching df.iloc[n] for scalar n. Supports negative positions.
  - row_to_series(usize): private helper extracting a row into a
    Series with mixed-dtype fallback to Utf8.

GroupBy — as_index support:
  - groupby_with_as_index(&[&str], bool): new public method on
    DataFrame that accepts an explicit as_index parameter. When true
    (the default), behaves identically to existing groupby(). When
    false, group keys become regular columns and the result index is
    a default integer range (0..n_groups), matching pandas
    df.groupby(by, as_index=False) semantics.
  - DataFrameGroupBy struct gains an as_index: bool field.
  - The aggregation path in DataFrameGroupBy now branches: when
    as_index is false, it prepends group key columns (extracted from
    the first row of each group) before the aggregated value columns,
    and assigns an Int64 range index.
  - Existing groupby() method now delegates to
    groupby_with_as_index(by, true), preserving full backward compat.

Tests — 30 new test functions covering:
  - Series loc_bool / iloc_bool: true-row selection, empty mask,
    wrong-length rejection, delegation from iloc to loc.
  - Series loc_slice / iloc_slice: inclusive endpoints, open start,
    open end, missing label rejection, exclusive end, negative
    positions, open-both-ends full return, out-of-range empty result.
  - DataFrame loc_bool / iloc_bool: true-row selection, wrong-length
    rejection, all-false empty result, delegation.
  - DataFrame loc_slice / iloc_slice: inclusive endpoints, open start,
    open end, missing label rejection, exclusive end, negative
    positions, out-of-range empty result.
  - DataFrame loc_row / iloc_row: series return shape, mixed dtype
    fallback to Utf8, missing label rejection, negative position,
    out-of-bounds rejection.
  - GroupBy as_index=false: sum aggregation, mean aggregation,
    as_index=true default backward-compat check.
  - Existing sort_index/sort_values tests are repositioned (not
    altered) to accommodate new test blocks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
